description_long: This is a medium-sized, open-topped wooden bucket or barrel with a slightly flared rim and a round base. The body is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each separated by visible seams, and is reinforced by two prominent dark metal hoops encircling the upper and lower sections. The wood has a rich, dark brown finish with subtle highlights, giving it a well-maintained appearance. The interior is hollow and appears to be unfinished wood, suitable for holding liquids or solid materials. There are no visible handles, lids, or decorative markings on the exterior. The overall design is utilitarian, suggesting it is intended for practical use in urban or garden settings.
description: A dark brown wooden bucket or barrel with two black metal hoops and an open top, featuring vertical staves and no handles or markings.
description_view_0: The bucket is seen from a slightly angled side, showing the flared rim, vertical staves, and both metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: This view presents the bucket almost directly from the front, emphasizing the symmetry and the even spacing of the staves and hoops.
description_view_2: The bucket is shown from a slightly different angle, with the curvature of the rim and the depth of the interior more visible.
description_view_3: This view is similar to view 1 but from the opposite side, again highlighting the uniformity of the staves and the placement of the hoops.
